request.description:
Retrieves the authentication configuration.

#### Request Parameters

None

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| notification_expiry_time | Integer | Returns the number of days in which the notifications expire. |
| activity_timeout | Bool | Returns True if the user logs out in case of inactivity, else returns False. |
| activity_timeout_time | Integer | Returns the minutes after which the user automatically will be logged out due to inactivity if enabled. |
| password_tries | Integer | Returns the maximum number of tries a user can attempt after which the user account will either be temporary or permanently blocked. |
| is_temporary_lockout | Bool | Returns True if the user has been blocked permanently or temporarily, else returns False. |
| lock_out_time | Integer | Returns the minutes after which the user account unblocks if blocked temporarily. |
| account_deactivation_enabled | Bool | Returns True if the user account blocks if not logged in for the configured days, else returns False. |
| max_inactive_days | Integer | Returns the maximum number of inactive days a user account can have before getting blocked. |
| concurrent_sessions | Bool | Returns True if a user can generate multiple authentication tokens at a time, else returns False. |
| jwt_token_expiry | Integer | Returns the expiration time of the JWT token. |
| two_factor_auth | Bool | Returns True if two-factor authentication is enabled, else returns False. |
| two_factor_auth_type | String | Returns the type of two-factor authentication enabled, such as email or TOTP. |
| allow_forgot_password_mail | Bool | Returns True if the forgot password policy has been enabled, else returns False. |
| otp_expiry_time | String | Returns the minutes after which OTP expires if sent over email. |
| password_link_expiry_time | Integer | Returns the time after which the reset password link expires if sent over email. |
| session_auth | Bool | Returns True if a user can open CTIX in multiple windows simultaneously, else returns False. |
| max_inactive_alert_days | Integer | Returns the number of inactive days, if a user has not logged into the application for a specific period of time as a reminder before the configured days expires. |
| recaptcha | Bool | Returns True if the re-captcha has been enabled, else returns False. |

response.name: Get Auth Config

    "account_deactivation_enabled": false,

}

request.description:
Retrieves the firewall rules defined for a Tenant. This API either allows all the requested IPs or Countries to access the application or block all the IPs or Countries.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any input parameters.

#### Response Parameters (Configuration Key)

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| restricted_access | Boolean | Returns True if the firewall is enabled, else returns False. |
| blacklist | Boolean | Returns True if specific traffic is allowed, else returns False. |

#### Response Parameters (Firewall List Key)

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | String | Returns the unique ID of the firewall rule. |
| reason | String | Returns the reason for adding a user to the rule. |
| is_active | Bool | Returns True if a rule has been applied on the traffic, else returns False. |
| type | String | Returns the type for which this rule has been defined. Rule type such as CIDR, Country. |
| value | String | Returns the value on which the rule has been applied. It can be IP or Country name based on type. |
| created_at | Integer | Returns the date and time of the rule creation. |
| updated_at | Integer | Returns the date and time when the rule has been updated. |

Retrieves details about Tenant component configuration.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any input parameters.

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| white_label | Boolean | Returns True if the Cyware logo has been hidden, else returns False. |
| max_upload_file_size | Integer | Returns the maximum file size user can upload. |
| tenant_notification | Boolean | Returns True if the tenant notifications have been enabled, else returns False. |
| mail_service_enabled | Boolean | Returns True if the mail service has been enabled, else returns False. |
| port_scanner_timeout | Integer | Returns the port scanner timeout. |
| timezone | String | Returns the tenant timezone. |
| logs_delete_time | Integer | Returns the number of days after which the logs get deleted. |

item.name: TAXII Server Configuration

request.description:
### GET Publishing Configuration

This endpoint retrieves the configuration details of the TAXII server on the platform.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any input parameter.

#### Response Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| max_polling_time | Integer | The maximum time (in seconds) the server will poll for new data. |
| poll_max_result_size | Integer | The maximum number of records returned in a single poll request. |
| live_graph_duration | Integer | The duration (in seconds) for which live graph data is available. |
| stix1 | Boolean | Indicates if STIX 1.1.1 format publishing is enabled. |
| stix1_2 | Boolean | Indicates if STIX 1.2 format publishing is enabled. |
| stix2 | Boolean | Indicates if STIX 2.0 format publishing is enabled. |
| stix2_v1 | Boolean | Indicates if STIX 2.1 format publishing is enabled. |
| misp | Boolean | Indicates if MISP format publishing is enabled. |
| default_content_binding | String | The default binding format for TAXII 1.x server. |
| auto_process_inbox_feed | Boolean | Indicates if the server automatically processes inbox feeds. |
| discovery_service_message | String | Message related to the discovery service. |
| poll_service_message | String | Message related to the poll service. |
| collection_service_message | String | Message related to the collection service. |
| inbox_service_message | String | Message related to the inbox service. |
| name_space_url | String | The URL reference for the name space. |
| name_space_name | String | The name identifier for STIX 1.x content. |

item.name: System Notifications

request.description:
Retrieves the system notification configuration for email feed sources.

#### Response Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| connection_failures | Object | Configuration for connection failure notifications |
| no_data_received | Object | Configuration for no data received notifications |

Each configuration object includes the following parameters:

- `is_enabled`: A boolean indicating if the notification is enabled.
    
- `is_notify_default`: A boolean indicating if the default notification is enabled.
    
- `is_notify_via`: An array of strings specifying the notification methods.
    
- `notify_before`: An integer specifying the time before notification.
    
- `notify_frequency`: A string specifying the frequency of notification.
    
- `user_groups`: An array of user groups for notification.
    
- `users`: An array of users for notification.

Retrieves the system notification configuration for internal failure reports.

#### Response Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| failed_rules | Object | Object containing configuration for failed rules |
| subscriber_error | Object | Object containing configuration for subscriber error |

Each configuration object includes the following parameters:

- `is_enabled`: A boolean indicating if the notification is enabled.
    
- `is_notify_default`: A boolean indicating if the default notification is enabled.
    
- `is_notify_via`: An array of strings specifying the notification methods.
    
- `notify_before`: An integer specifying the time before notification.
    
- `notify_frequency`: A string specifying the frequency of notification.
    
- `user_groups`: An array of user groups for notification.
    
- `users`: An array of users for notification.

Retrieves the system notification configuration for the OpenAPI expiry report.

### Response

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| openapi.is_enabled | boolean | Indicates if the OpenAPI notification is enabled. |
| openapi.is_notify_default | boolean | Indicates if the default notification is enabled. |
| openapi.is_notify_via | array | List of notification methods. |
| openapi.notify_before | integer | Number of minutes before notification. |
| openapi.notify_frequency | string | Frequency of notification. |
| openapi.user_groups | array | List of user groups to be notified. |
| openapi.users | array | List of users to be notified. |

item.description: You can configure the system notifications of the platform to send timely in-app and email notifications to relevant users about errors related to feed sources, subscribers, rules, and API credentials. The notifications enable the users to take proactive measures and avoid any disruption in the platform.

item.name: Custom Scores

request.description:
Retrieves all the custom scores.

## Request Parameters

This endpoint does not require any request parameters.

## Response Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| results | Array of JSON Objects | Returns an array of custom scores. |
| id | String | Returns the ID of the custom score. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the custom score. |
| api_key | String | Returns the API key of the custom score. |
| helptext | String | Returns the help text of the custom score. |
| field_type | String | Returns the field type. Possible values: `integer`, `select`, `text`. |
| values | Array of strings | Returns a list of allowed field values. Applicable for the `select` field type. |
| is_deprecated | Boolean | Returns true if the custom score is deprecated, else false. |

response.name: Retrieve Custom Scores

header.value: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS

request.description:
Creates a new custom score. You can add maximum of five custom scores. ### Request Parameters | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | name | String | Required | Pass the name of the custom score. | | helptext | String | Optional | Pass the help text for the custom score. | | field_type | String | Required | Pass the type of field for the custom score. You can specify one of the following field type:
`integer`: Allows you to enter a text value for the custom score.
`text`: Allows you to enter an integer value for the custom score.
`select`: Allows you to select one of the pre-defined list of values for the custom score. | | values | Array of strings | Optional | Pass a list of allowed values for the custom score. Required if field type is `select`. | ### Response Returns success message after creating the custom score.

header.value: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS

variable.description: Pass the ID of a custom score to update

request.description:
Updates a custom score.

#### Request Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| name | String | Optional | Pass the name of the custom score. |
| helptext | String | Optional | Pass the help text for the custom score. |
| values | Array of strings | Optional | Pass the array of values for the custom score. Required if the field type is single select list. |
| is_deprecated | Boolean | Optional | Pass true to set the custom score as deprecated, else false. |

#### Response Parameters

Returns success message if updated successfully.

item.name: Collection Management

query.description: Filter results by page number.
Filter results by the page size of response.
Pass -created to sort by created date in descending order. Pass created to sort by created date in ascending order.
Pass true to retrieve active collections.
Pass the created from time to retrieve collection based on the created date.
Pass the created to time to retrieve collection based on the created date.
Pass the type of collection. Allowed values: inbox, polling
Pass a keyword to search collections based on the title.
Pass true to retrieve collections with the ID and name of the collections.

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of collections in CTIX.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any input parameters.

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| next | String | Returns the link to the next page |
| previous | String | Returns the link to the previous page |
| page_size | Integer | Returns the maximum number of elements that can be present on a page. |
| total | Integer | Returns the total record count. |
| results | Array of Objects | Returns the list of collection objects. See the Collection Parameters table for details |

#### **Collection Parameters**

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | String | Returns the collection ID. |
| name | String | Returns the collection name. |
| description | String | Returns the collection description. |
| is_active | Boolean | Returns true if the collection is active, else returns false. |
| type | String | Returns the type of collection. |
| is_editable | Boolean | Returns true if the collection is editable, else returns false. |
| polling | Boolean | Returns true if the polling is allowed, else returns false. |
| inbox | Boolean | Returns true if the inbox is allowed for collection, else returns false. |
| created | Timestamp | Returns the date and time at which the collection was created. |
| has_subscribed | Boolean | Returns true if the collection is subscribed by any subscriber, else returns false. |
| marking_config | String | Returns the data marking type. |
| default_marking_definition | Object | Returns the default data marking configuration. |

response.name: List Collections

query.description: Filter results by page number.

Pass the created from time to retrieve collection based on the created date.
Pass the created to time to retrieve collection based on the created date.
Pass the type of collection. Allowed values: inbox, polling
Pass a keyword to search collections based on the title.

request.description:
Retrieves all tags from Intel Exchange.

### Request Parameters

This endpoint does not require any request parameters.

### Response Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| next | String | Returns a link to the next page. |
| page_size | Integer | Returns the number of tags reterived per page. |
| previous | String | Returns the link to the previous page. |
| results | Array of JSON Objects | Returns an array of results which consists of tags in JSON objects |
| colour_code | String | Returns the hex key of the colour code which denotes type of the tag. |
| created | Timestamp | Returns the EPOCH time of tag creation. |
| created_by | JSON Object | Returns the details of the user who created the tag. |
| id | String | Returns the tag ID. |
| is_active | Boolean | Returns true if the tag is enabled, else false. |
| modified | Timestamp | Retuns the last modified time in EPOCH format. |
| modified_by | JSON Object | Returns the details of the user who last modified the tag. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the tag. |
| tag_type | JSON Object | Returns details of the tag type such as tag ID and tag name. |
| user_groups | Array of JSON Objects | Returns the array of user groups associated with tag. Applicable for privilaged access tags. |
| total | Integer | Returns the total number of tags. |

request.description:
Create multiple tags with one request. You can create a maximum of 15 tags in one API request. ### Request Parameters | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | name | String | Required | Pass a list of tag names separated by a comma within 5000 characters. | | tag_type | String | Required | Pass the type of the tag. You can create one of the following tags:
`user`: Tags created by the Intel Exchange users
`system`: Tags that the users create for use within Intel Exchange.
`privilaged`: Tags that enables you to restrict user access to the associated threat data objects. | | user_groups | Array | Optional
Required if `tag_type=privilaged` | Pass an array of user groups associated with the tag. Applicable for privilaged access tags. | | action | String | Required | Pass the action to be performed on the tags. Allowed values: `add`. | ### Response Parameters | Parameter | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | result | JSON Object | Returns the result of the bulk action. | | details | String | Returns the details of the action performed. | | count | Integer | Returns the number of tags created. |

item.description: Tags are labels or keywords used to categorize and organize threat intel. They help classify and group related data, making it easier to search, filter, and manage the information within the platform. From user-defined tags to source, system, and privileged access tags, this provides a seamless platform to organize and administer tag categories.
Learn about the APIs to manage the administrative features such as integrations, configurations, usermanagement, and more.

item.name: Quick Add Intel

request.description:
Creates an IOC parsing task to extract STIX objects from free text data or a web page. Currently, this API endpoint does not support parsing of IOCs from files, such as PDF, CSV, XLS, and other files. #### **Request Parameters** Pass the following parameters in the payload of an API request. | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | data | String | Mandatory | Pass the free text data within 50,000 characters or the URL of a web page to extract STIX objects.
If the number of characters in the passed text data or the web page exceeds the character limit, then the initial 50,000 characters are parsed. | | url | Boolean | Optional | Pass `true` to extract STIX objects from a web page.
If you pass `false`, then the URL passed in the `data` parameter is parsed as free text. | #### Response Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | task_id | String | Returns the ID of the parsing task.
To retrieve the parsing task status, use the _GET Retrieve Parse IOCs Task Status_ API. |

query.description: [Required] Pass 'status' to retrieve the status of a task. Allowed values: status
Pass 'true' to retrieve the numeric status  of the task.

variable.description: Enter the ID of a parse IOCs task.

request.description:
Returns the status of a parse IOCs task.

#### **Request Parameters**

No parameter.

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| status | String | Returns the status of the parsing task. |

The following table describes various status of a parsing task.

| Numeric Status | Status Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | enqueued | The parsing task is in the queue for processing. |
| 1 | running | The parsing task is being processed. |
| 2 | failed | Failed to process parsing due to invalid input. |
| 3 | done | The parsing task has been successfully completed. Use the _GET Retrieve Parsed IOCs_ API to retrieve the parsed IOCs. |
| 5 | delayed | Parsing is delayed due to too many tasks in the queue. Try again after sometime. |
| 6 | canceled | Parsing has been manually cancelled by the user. |
| 7 | aborted | Parsing aborted due to some unexpected error. Create a new parsing task. |

response.name: Get parsed IOCs task status - status slug

request.description:
Retrieves the result after successfully processing the _POST Create Parse IOCs Task API_.

Returns a list of parsed IOCs as STIX objects. Also, returns whether an IOC exists in the Allowed Indicators list in the platform.

## Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

## Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| as | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed autonomous systems. |
| ips | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed IPv4 addresses. |
| emails | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed email addresses. |
| urls | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed URLs. |
| sha256 | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed SHA256 hashes. |
| md5 | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed MD5 hashes. |
| domains | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed domains. |
| sha1 | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed SHA1 hashes. |
| sha224 | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed SHA224 hashes. |
| sha384 | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed SHA384 hashes. |
| sha512 | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed SHA512 hashes. |
| ssdeeps | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed SSDEEP hashes. |
| cve_ids | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed CVE IDs. |
| registry_key_path | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed Windows registry keys. |
| file_paths | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed file paths. |
| directory | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed directories. |
| mac-addr | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed MAC addresses. |
| x509-certificate | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed X.509 certificates. |
| char_limit_exceeded | Boolean | Returns `true` if the number of characters passed in the payload for parsing exceeds 50,000 characters. |
| description | String | Returns the text data passed in the payload for parsing. |

Each parsed IOC object of the response includes the following parameters:

| **Parameter** | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| value | String | Returns the value of an IOC. |
| is_whitelisted | Boolean | Returns `true` if an IOC exists in the Allowed Indicators list in the platform. |

request.description:
Returns a list of intel added using quick add intel.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| next | String | Returns a link to the next page of the intel history. |
| previous | String | Returns a link to the previous page of the intel history. |
| page_size | Integer | Returns the number of records per page. |
| total | Integer | Returns the number of records available in the quick add history. |
| results | List | Returns the details of the quick add intel history. |

#### The Result Objects

The objects of the Quick Add Intel History include the following parameters.

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| client_collections | Array | Returns a list of client collections in the platform. For quick add intel submissions, this value is `null`. |
| component | String | Returns the component of the intel submission. For quick add intel submission, the value is `quick-add-intel`. |
| created | Timestamp | Returns the created date of the intel. |
| created_by | JSON Object | Returns the details of the creator of the intel. |
| error_message | String | Returns an error message if the intel creation fails. Else, returns null. |
| feed_id | String | Returns the ID of the intel source. For Quick Add Intel, the default source is Import. |
| id | String | Returns the ID of the quick add intel submission. |
| metadata | JSON Object | Returns the metadata details of the intel, such as the Confidence Score, tags, and description. |
| modified | Timestamp | Returns the last modified date of the intel. |
| modified_by | JSON Object | Returns the details of the modifier of the intel. |
| published_on | Timestamp | Returns the published date of the intel to a STIX collection in the platform. |
| report_id | String | Returns the ID of the report objected that is created as part of the quick add intel submission. |
| server_collections | Array | Returns a list of server collections in the platform. For quick add intel submissions, this value is `null`. |
| status | String | Returns the intel creation status. |
| title | String | Returns the title of the intel. |
| tlp | String | Returns the TLP of the intel. |

request.description:
Returns the statistics of Quick Add Intel history.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

#### Response Parameters

Returns the status of the intel and the respective number of intel submissions.

Creates intel by providing minimal details of indicators, SDOs, relations, custom objects, and indicators parsed from free text. Default Source: Import Default Collection: Free Text #### **Request Parameters** | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | create_intel_feed | Boolean | Optional | Pass `true` to create an intel feed. Else, pass `false` to create a draft intel.
Default value: `false` | | title | String | Mandatory | Pass a title for the intel within 100 characters. The title is added to the report object that is created as part of the quick add intel submission. | | indicators | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the IOC types and IOC values in key-value pairs. For more information, see _The Indicators Object_. | | metadata | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the additional information to add to the intel. | | metadata.confidence | Integer | Optional | Pass a Source Confidence Score for the intel between 0 and 100. | | metadata.tlp | String | Optional | Pass TLP value for the intel.
Allowed values:
`GREEN`
`AMBER`
`RED`
`WHITE`
`NONE` | | metadata.custom_scores | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the custom score key and the score in key value pairs.
To retrieve the keys of custom scores, use the _GET Custom Scores_ API in the Administration > Configuration > Custom Scores section. | | metadata.description | String | Optional | Pass a description for the intel within 1000 characters. | | metadata.labels | Array | Optional | Pass a list of tag IDs to add to the intel.
To retrieve the list of tags, use the _GET List Tags_ API in the _Tags_ section. | | metadata.is_apply_all | Boolean | Optional | Pass `true` to apply the metadata to all objects of the intel. Else, pass `false` to apply the metadata to the report object that is created as part of the quick add intel submission only.
Default value: `false` | | parse_indicators | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the IOC types and indicators parsed from free text. For more information, see _The Parse Indicators Object_. | | custom_objects | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the ID of the custom objects and the corresponding custom attributes. For more information, see _The Custom Objects Object_. | | sdos | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the SDO types and comma-separated SDO values. For more information, see _The SDOs Object_. | | observables | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the STIX Cyber Observable Object (SCO) types and comma-separated SCO values. For more information, see _The Observables Object_. | | relations | Array of JSON Objects | Optional | Pass the objects to be associated with the report object created as part of this Quick Add submission.
You can associate a maximum of 10 objects in a single quick add submission.

The following object types are not supported for adding as relations:
**Domain Objects**: Observed Data, Opinion, Report, Note, Custom Object, and Location
**Observables**: Network Traffic and X.509 Certificate
**Indicators**: MD5, SHA1, SHA224, SHA384, SHA256, SHA512, and SSDEEP | | relations.{object index}.name | String | Required | Pass the value of the object. | | relations.{object index}.type | String | Required | Pass the object type. | | relations.{object index}.ioc_type | String | Required if the object type is an indicator | Pass the IOC type if the object type is an indicator | | relations.{object index}.id | String | Required | Pass the unique ID of the object. | **Note**: Atleast one of indicators, sdos, observables, or custom_objects is required to create an intel. #### The Indicators Object The `indicators` object in Quick Add Intel accepts the IOC type as the key and a comma-separated list of IOC values as the value. You can pass a maximum of 5000 characters as the IOC value per IOC type. For the allowed list of IOC types and the respective key to pass in the `indicators` object, refer to the following table: | **IOC Type** | **IOC Key** | | --- | --- | | IPv4 | `ipv4-addr` | | IPv6 | `ipv6-addr` | | Domain | `domain` | | URL | `url` | | Email Address | `email` | | MD5 | `md5` | | SHA1 | `sha1` | | SHA224 | `sha224` | | SHA256 | `sha256` | | SHA384 | `sha384` | | SHA512 | `sha512` | | SSDEEP | `ssdeep` | #### The Parse Indicators Object The `parse_indicators` object in Quick Add Intel accepts the IOC type as the key and a list of parsed IOC values as the value. For the allowed list of IOC types and the respective key to pass in the `parse_indicators` object, refer to the following table: | IOC Type | IOC Key | | --- | --- | | IPv4 | `ips` | | IPv6 | `ipv6` | | Domain | `domains` | | URL | `urls` | | Email Address | `emails` | | MD5 | `md5` | | SHA1 | `sha1` | | SHA224 | `sha224` | | SHA256 | `sha256` | | SHA384 | `sha384` | | SHA512 | `sha512` | | SSDEEP | `ssdeep` | | CVE IDs | `cve_ids` | | Windows Registry Key | `registry_key_path` | | File Paths | `file_paths` | | Autonomous System | `as` | | X.509 certificates | `x509-certificate` | | MAC Address | `mac-addr` | #### The Custom Objects Object The `custom_objects` object in Quick Add Intel accepts the ID of a custom object as the key and a list of custom attribute objects as the value. The custom attribute object accepts the name of the primary attribute of a custom object as the key and the attribute value as the value. To retrieve the custom object IDs and the respective primary attribute, use the _GET Custom Object Details_ API in **Administration** > **Custom Entitites Management** > **Custom Objects**. #### The SDOs Object The `sdos` object in Quick Add Intel accepts the STIX Domain Object (SDO) type as the key and a comma-separated list of SDO values as the value. You can pass a maximum of 5000 characters as the SDO value per SDO type. For the allowed list of SDO types and the respective key to pass in the `sdos` object, refer to the following table. | SDO Type | SDO Key | | --- | --- | | Vulnerability | `vulnerability` | | Malware | `malware` | | Campaign | `campaign` | | Threat Actor | `threat-actor` | | Intrusion Set | `intrusion-set` | | Attack Pattern | `attack-pattern` | | Incident | `incident` | | Course of Action | `course-of-action` | | Identity | `identity` | | Tool | `tool` | | Infrastructure | `infrastructure` | | Location | `location` | | Malware Analysis | `malware-analysis` | #### The Observables Object The `observables` object in Quick Add Intel accepts the STIX Cyber Observable Object (SCO) type as the key and a comma-separated list of SCO values as the value. You can pass a maximum of 5000 characters as the SCO value per SCO type. For the allowed list of SCO types and the respective key to pass in the `observables` object, refer to the following table. | SCO Type | SCO Key | | --- | --- | | Artifact | `artifact` | | Directory | `directory` | | Email Message | `email-message` | | MAC Address | `mac-addr` | | Mutex | `mutex` | | ​Network Traffic | `network-traffic` | | Process | `process` | | Software | `software` | | User Account | `user-account` | | X.509 Certificate | `x509-certificate` | | File | `file` | #### Response Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | details | String | Returns that the details are successfully added to the intel creation queue. | | task_id | String | Returns the unique ID of the background task that is created to track the intel creation status. |

Possible values:

`DELETED`
`FAILED`
`PARTIALLY_SUCCESSFUL` | | report_id | String | Returns the unique ID of the report object that is created as part of the quick add intel submission. |

query.description: Pass the number of objects to retrieve. Default: 10
Pass the keyword to search based on the object name

variable.description: Pass the unique ID of a report object to retrieve the related objects.

request.description:
Retrieve the details of the threat objects (indicators, SDOs, SCOs, relations, and custom objects) that are ingested and added as relationship objects of the report that is created as part of the quick add intel submission. #### Request Parameters None #### Response Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | next | String | Returns a link to the next page of the response. | | page_size | Integer | Returns the number of objects returned per page. | | previous | String | Returns a link to the previous page of the response. | | results | Array of JSON Objects | Returns a list of JSON objects for each threat data object. | | is_redacted | Boolean | Returns true if the object includes restricted tags.
If is_redacted=true, then the name of related object is masked. | | relationship_type | String | Returns the relationship type of the object with the report object. | | sources | Array of JSON Objects | Returns a list of JSON objects for each source of the threat data object. | | target_ref | JSON Object | Returns the details of the object ingested as part of the quick add intel submission. | | total | Integer | Returns the total number of objects related to the report object. |

item.description:
Quick Add Intel enables you to quickly add intel into CTIX by providing some basic details.

This section describes how to use the API endpoints related to Quick Add Intel.

item.name: Import Intel

formdata.description: Select the file to be imported.

(Optional) Enter the ID of the collection to which the file is imported. Example, 603dd2cf-2c3e-4a6b-8200-505d3586df1f. To retrieve the collection_id, use the GET Collections API. Data is imported to both the default collection as well as this specified collection.

query.description: If the format (path variable) value is stix1 or stix2, enter the STIX version. Default value is 2.1. Allowed values are 1.0, 2.0, and 2.1

variable.description: Enter the format for the import. Allowed values are cy-csv, misp, openioc, stix1, stix2, stix20, stix1url, and csv-recorded-future.

request.description:
This endpoint imports threat data to Intel Exchange. The formats supported for import are as follows:

| Format | Value | **Allowed File Type** | **Default Collection** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| MISP | misp | .json | misp |
| Open IOC | openioc | .xml | openioc |
| STIX 1.x | stix1 | .xml | xml |
| STIX 2.1 | stix2 | .json | Stix2 |
| STIX 2.0 | stix20 | .json | Stix2 |
| STIX 1.x URL | stix1url | URL | url |
| CSV (Recorded Future) | csv-recorded-future | .csv | csv |
| CSV (Cyware) | cy-csv | .csv | csv |

Optionally, you can specify a collection to which the file is imported. If no collection is specified, the data is imported to the default collection.  
To retrieve import collections, use the **GET Collections** API.

Note: For STIX 1.x URL, the body must include the URL value. For more information, see example request.

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| details | String | Retrieves the import status details. To confirm if the intel was successfully imported, sign in to the Intel Exchange platform and check the Notifications section. |

response.name: Import Intel (STIX URL)
Import Intel (STIX2)

formdata.description: Select the file to be imported.
formdata.fileNotInWorkingDirectoryWarning: This file isn't in your working directory. Teammates you share this request with won't be able to use this file. To make collaboration easier you can setup your working directory in Settings.

formdata.description: (Optional) Enter the ID of the collection to which the file is imported. Example, 603dd2cf-2c3e-4a6b-8200-505d3586df1f. To retrieve the collection_id, use the GET Collections API. Data is imported to both the default collection as well as this specified collection.

query.description: If the format (path variable) value is stix1 or stix2, enter the STIX version. Allowed values are 1.0, 2.0, and 2.1

variable.description: Enter the format for the import. Allowed values are cy-csv, cyware-csv, freetext, maec, misp, openioc, pdffreetext, stix1, stix2, stix20, stix1url, yara, and csv-recorded-future.

query.description: Pass true to fetch import collections. Default value is true.
Pass the number of collection records to be retrieved. Default value is 10. Example, 5.
Pass the page number. Default value is 1.

request.description:
This endpoint retrieves import collections from Intel Exchange.

#### Response Parameters

The response will contain an array of collections with the following fields:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | String | The unique identifier of the collection |
| name | String | The name of the collection |
| host | String | The host associated with the collection |
| source | Object | Information about the source, including its ID and name. |
| start_time | Integer | The start time for the polling. |
| subscribed | Boolean | Indicates if the collection is subscribed. |
| subscribed_once | Boolean | Indicates if the collection has been subscribed to at least once. |
| status | Integer | The polling status of the collection. |
| created | Integer | Timestamp of when the collection was created. |
| modified | Integer | Timestamp of when the collection was modified. |
| taxii_version | String | The TAXII version of the collection. |
| subscriber_id | string | The unique identifier of the subscriber. |
| inbox | Object | Information about the inbox, including its status and url. |
| polling | Object | Information about polling, including its status, polled_once, type, url, url_choices, polling_time, and last_polling_time |

response.name: Collections

header.value: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS

item.description: You can import intel from structured data sources such as STIX bundles, CSV files, and OpenIOC into ​Intel Exchange (CTIX)​​ and use these imported sources to create intel within the platform.

item.name: Create Intel via Open API
Ingest Bundle

request.description:
# Ingest Bundle with Source and Collection Name

Use this API to ingest a STIX bundle into Intel Exchange by specifying the source and collection name. A STIX bundle can include any combination of STIX Domain Objects (SDOs). For more information on supported objects, see the [STIX documentation](https://docs.oasis-open.org/cti/stix/v2.1/csprd01/stix-v2.1-csprd01.html).

**Note**: If you pass an invalid or missing `source_name` or `collection_name`, the bundle is ingested with the source set to **Import** and the collection set to **STIX2**.

## Request Parameters

| Parameter Key | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| bundle | object | Yes | Pass the STIX bundle that contains one or more STIX objects to ingest. |
| bundle.id | string | Yes | Pass a unique identifier for the bundle being ingested. |
| bundle.objects | array | Yes | Pass an array of STIX objects included in the bundle. |
| bundle.objects.type | string | Yes | Pass the object type. For example, `indicator`, `malware`, `threat-actor`. |
| bundle.objects.spec_version | string | Yes | Pass the STIX specification version used for the object. |
| bundle.objects.id | string | Yes | Pass a unique identifier for the specific STIX object. |
| bundle.objects.created | string | Yes | Pass the timestamp when the object was created. |
| bundle.objects.modified | string | Yes | Pass the timestamp when the object was last modified. |
| bundle.objects.name | string | No | Pass a human-readable name of the object. |
| bundle.objects.indicator_types | array | Yes | Pass the classification types for the indicator. For example, `anomalous-activity`. |
| bundle.objects.pattern | string | Yes | Pass the detection pattern that defines the indicator’s characteristics. For example, `[url:value = 'http: //affected-domains.com/test']`. |
| bundle.objects.pattern_type | string | Yes | Pass the type of pattern used. For example, `stix`. |
| bundle.objects.pattern_version | string | No | Pass the version of the pattern specification being used. |
| bundle.objects.valid_from | string | Yes | Pass the timestamp from which the indicator is considered valid. |
| bundle.objects.object_marking_refs | array | Yes | Pass an array of marking definition references that specify the handling or sharing restrictions for the object. |
| bundle.type | string | Yes | Pass the object type of the bundle. |
| collection_name | string | Yes | Pass the name of the collection that should receive the bundle. |
| source_name | string | Yes | Pass the name of the source from which the bundle originates. |
| source_type | string | Yes | Pass the type of the source. For example, `CUSTOM_STIX_SOURCES`. |

## Response Parameters

| Parameter Key | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| success | boolean | Returns whether the bundle ingestion was successful. |
| message | string | Returns additional details about the ingestion process. |
| data | object | Returns any data generated during the ingestion, if applicable. |
| error_code | string | Returns the error code if the ingestion fails. |
| error_message | string | Returns a description of the error if the ingestion fails. |

# Ingest Bundle with Source and Collection ID

Use this API to ingest a STIX bundle into Intel Exchange by specifying the source and collection ID. A STIX bundle can include any combination of STIX Domain Objects (SDOs). For more information on supported objects, see the [STIX documentation](https://docs.oasis-open.org/cti/stix/v2.1/csprd01/stix-v2.1-csprd01.html).

**Note**: If you pass an invalid or missing `source_id` or `collection_id`, the bundle is ingested with the source set to **Import** and the collection set to **STIX2**.

## Request Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| source_id | string | Yes | Pass the unique identifier for the source of the bundle being ingested. |
| source_type | string | Yes | Pass the type of the source. For example, `CUSTOM_STIX_SOURCES`. |
| collection_id | string | Yes | Pass the unique identifier for the collection that should recieve the bundle. |
| bundle | object | Yes | Pass the STIX bundle that includes one or more STIX objects. |
| bundle.type | string | Yes | Pass the type of the bundle. |
| bundle.id | string | Yes | Pass a unique identifier for the bundle. |
| bundle.objects | array | Yes | Pass an array of STIX objects contained in the bundle. |
| bundle.objects.type | string | Yes | Pass the object type. For example, `indicator`, `malware`, `threat_actor`. |
| bundle.objects.spec_version | string | Yes | Pass the STIX specification version used for the object. |
| bundle.objects.id | string | Yes | Pass a unique identifier for the specific STIX object. |
| bundle.objects.created | string | Yes | Pass the timestamp when the object was created. |
| bundle.objects.modified | string | Yes | Pass the timestamp when the object was last modified. |
| bundle.objects.name | string | No | Pass a human-readable name or description of the object. |
| bundle.objects.pattern | string | Yes | Pass the detection pattern that defines the indicator's characteristics. For example, `[ipv4-addr:value = '205.165.206.118']`. |
| bundle.obejects.pattern_type | string | Yes | Pass the type of pattern used. For example, `stix`. |
| bundle.objects.pattern_version | string | No | Pass the version of the pattern specification. For example, `2.1`. |
| bundle.objects.valid_from | string | Yes | Pass the timestamp from which the indicator is considered valid. |

## Response Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| success | boolean | Returns whether the bundle ingestion was successful. |
| message | string | Returns additional details about the ingestion process. |
| data | object | Returns any data generated during the ingestion. |
| error_code | string | Returns the error code if the ingestion fails. |
| error_message | string | Returns a description of the error if the ingestion fails. |

### Example Response

``` json
{"success":true,"message":""}

 ```

item.description:
The Ingest Bundle endpoint allows you to import threat intelligence data into Intel Exchange in the STIX format. Using this endpoint, you can directly ingest STIX bundles from external sources into collections for further enrichment and analysis.

You can ingest bundles in two ways:

- With Source and Collection Name: Ingest data by specifying the STIX source and collection name.
    
- With Source and Collection ID: Ingest data by referencing the unique collection ID linked to a STIX source.

request.description:
Create intel with additional details such as the source and collection. You can also include details for each object, such as description, notes, custom attributes, and more. #### Request Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | source | String | Optional | Pass the name of the source to map the intel.
If the passed source name does not exists in the platform, then a new source is automatically created in the Miscellaneous source category.
Default source: `Miscellaneous` of the `Miscellaneous` source category. | | collection | String | Optional | Pass the name of the source collection to associate with the source.
If the passed collection name does not exists in the platform, then a new collection is automatically created.
Default collection: Free Text | | metadata | JSON Object | Optional | Pass additional information to associate with the intel. | | metadata.confidence | Integer | Optional | Pass the Source Confidence Score to associate with the intel.
Default value: null | | metadata.tlp | String | Optional | Pass the TLP to associate with the intel.
Allowed values:
`GREEN`
`AMBER`
`RED`
`WHITE`
`NONE`
Default value: null | | metadata.labels | Array | Optional | Pass a list of tag names to associate with the intel.
To retrieve the tags, use the _GET List Tags_ API. | | title | String | Mandatory | Pass the title of the intel within 100 characters. | | all_sdos | Dictionary | Optional | Pass the SDO and IOC objects to add to the intel. For more information, see The _All SDOs Object._ | #### The All SDOs Object The `all_sdos` object accepts `all_iocs` and the SDO type as the key and a list of objects for the respective IOC and SDO type. For a list of the supported SDO types, refer to the following table. | SDO Type | SDO Key | | --- | --- | | Vulnerability | `vulnerabilities` | | Malware | `malwares` | | Campaign | `campaigns` | | Threat Actor | `threat_actors` | | Intrusion Set | `intrusion_sets` | | Attack Pattern | `attack_patterns` | | Incident | `incidents` | | Course of Action | `course_of_actions` | | Identity | `identities` | | Tool | `tools` | | Infrastructure | `infrastructures` | | Location | `locations` | | Malware Analysis | `malware_analysis` | | Custom Objects | `custom_objects` | | File | `files` | | Grouping | `groupings` | | Indicator | `indicators` | | Marking Definition | `marking_definitions` | | Note | `notes` | | Observed Data | `observed_datas` | | Opinion | `opinions` | | Relationship | `relationships` | | Observables | `cyber_observables` | | Software | `softwares` | #### The ALL IOCs Object The `all_iocs` object accepts the IOC type as the key and a list of objects for the respective IOC ype. For a list of the supported IOC types, refer to the following table. | IOC Type | IOC Key | | --- | --- | | IPv4 | `ipv4` | | IPv6 | `ipv6` | | Domain | `domain` | | URL | `url` | | Email Address | `email_address` | | MD5 | `md5` | | SHA1 | `sha1` | | SHA224 | `sha224` | | SHA256 | `sha256` | | SHA384 | `sha384` | | SHA512 | `sha512` | | SSDEEP | `ssdeep` | #### The IOC/SDO Object Pass the following parameters in the JSON object of every IOC or SDO you want add. | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | value | String | Mandatory | Pass the value of an IOC or SDO. | | description | String | Optional | Pass the description of the IOC or SDO within 5000 characters. | | is_false_positive | Boolean | Optional | Pass `true` to mark the IOC or SDO as False-Positive. Default value: `false` | | notes | Array | Optional | Pass a list of note strings to add to the IOC or SDO. | | custom_attributes | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the custom attribute names and the attribute values in key-value pairs.
**Note**: The custom attribute name must start with `x_`. For example, `x_identity` | #### Response Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | details | String | Returns the following success message:
"Intel creation is in progress." |

item.description: Create intel with additional details such as the source and collection to better group and correlate data.

item.name: Threat Data
Export

request.description:
Initiates export of threat data objects to a CSV file based on the CQL input. This API returns a file ID to retrieve the export file download link. You can export a maximum of 100,000 threat data objects in one API request. **Request Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | format | String | Mandatory | Pass the export file format.
Currently, CTIX supports only the `csv` format. | | component | String | Mandatory | Pass the component you want to export.
Currently, CTIX supports only `threat_data`. | | query | String | Mandatory | Pass the CQL query to filter threat data objects.
**Tip**: You can copy the CQL from the CTIX user interface. Press the following keys to copy the CQL code to use in the API request payload:
Windows: Windows + Shift + C
MacOS: Command + Shift + C | | columns | Array | Mandatory | Pass the list of columns you want to export.
For the allowed list of columns, see _The Column Object_. | **The Column Object** Each column object includes the key and value of a column. You can include the following columns in the export file. | **Key** | **Value** | | --- | --- | | name | Value | | type | Type | | tlp | TLP | | tags | Tags | | sources | Source | | source_collections | Source Collection | | published_collections | Published Collections | | ctix_created | System Created Date | | ctix_modified | System Modified Date | | country | Country | | analyst_score | Analyst Score | | source_confidence | Source Confidence | **Response Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | details | String | Returns a message that export of the threat data objects is initiated. | | file_id | String | Returns the ID of the export file. You can use this ID to retrieve the download link. |

variable.description:

request.description:
Deprecate or undeprecate multiple threat data objects with one API request.

#### Request Parameter

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| object_ids | Array | Mandatory | Pass the list of threat data object IDs to perform the bulk action. To retrieve a list of object IDs, use the _List Threat Data API_ under the Threat Data section. |
| object_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of threat data objects. To get the list of supported object types, see _Supported SDO Types_ in Threat Data > Miscellaneous. |

#### Response Parameters

Status code 200: Action Successfully Executed

Status code 400: Bad Request (Check if you have passed valid data)

variable.description:
Pass manual_review to mark objects for manual review.
Pass reviewed to mark objects as reviewed.

| object_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of threat data objects. To get the list of supported object types, see _Supported SDO Types_ in Threat Data > Miscellaneous. |

| object_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of threat data objects. To get the list of supported object types, see _Supported SDO Types_ in Threat Data > Miscellaneous. |

request.description:

#### Request Parameter

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| object_ids | Array | Mandatory | Pass the list of threat data object IDs to perform the bulk action. To retrieve a list of object IDs, use the _List Threat Data API_ under the Threat Data section. |
| object_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of threat data objects. To get the list of supported object types, see _Supported SDO Types_ in Threat Data > Miscellaneous. |

#### Response Parameters

Status code 200: Action Successfully Executed

Status code 400: Bad Request (Check if you have passed valid data)

Add an analyst TLP to multiple threat data objects. #### Request Parameter | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | object_ids | Array | Mandatory | Pass the list of threat data object IDs to perform the bulk action. To retrieve a list of object IDs, use the _List Threat Data API_ under the Threat Data section. | | object_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of threat data objects. To get the list of supported object types, see _Supported SDO Types_ in Threat Data > Miscellaneous. | | data | JSON Object | Mandatory | Pass the additional data to pass with the request. | | data.analyst_tlp | String | Mandatory | Pass the analyst TLP to add the objects.
Allowed values:
`WHITE`
`GREEN`
`AMBER`
`RED` | #### Response Parameters Status code 200: Action Successfully Executed Status code 400: Bad Request (Check if you have passed valid data)

variable.description:
Pass add_tag to add tags to objects.
Pass remove_tags to remove tags from objects.

request.description:
Add or remove tags from multiple threat data objects.

You can remove a maximum of 50 tags from a maximum of 100 objects with one API request.

#### Request Parameter

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| object_ids | Array | Mandatory | Pass the list of threat data object IDs to perform the bulk action. To retrieve a list of object IDs, use the _List Threat Data API_ under the Threat Data section. |
| object_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of threat data objects. To get the list of supported object types, see _Supported SDO Types_ in Threat Data > Miscellaneous. |
| data | JSON Object | Mandatory | Pass the additional data to pass with the request. |
| data.tag_id | Array | Mandatory | Pass the ID of the tags to be added or removed. To retrieve the tag IDs, use the _List Tags_ API. |

#### Response Parameters

Status code 200: Action Successfully Executed

Status code 400: Bad Request (Check if you have passed valid data)

Add an analyst Confidence Score to multiple threat data objects.

#### Request Parameter

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| object_ids | Array | Mandatory | Pass the list of threat data object IDs to perform the bulk action. To retrieve a list of object IDs, use the _List Threat Data API_ under the Threat Data section. |
| object_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of threat data objects. To get the list of supported object types, see _Supported SDO Types_ in Threat Data > Miscellaneous. |
| data | JSON Object | Mandatory | Pass the additional data to pass with the request. |
| data.analyst_score | Integer | Mandatory | Pass the analyst Confidence Score between 0 to 100 to add the objects. |

#### Response Parameters

Status code 200: Action Successfully Executed

Status code 400: Bad Request (Check if you have passed valid data)

Add a relation to multiple threat data objects. #### Request Parameter | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | object_ids | Array | Mandatory | Pass the list of threat data object IDs to perform the bulk action. To retrieve a list of object IDs, use the _List Threat Data API_ under the Threat Data section. | | object_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of threat data objects. To get the list of supported object types, see _Supported SDO Types_ in Threat Data > Miscellaneous. | | data | JSON Object | Mandatory | Pass the additional data to pass with the request. | | data.target | JSON Object | Mandatory | Pass the details of the object to add as a relation. | | data.target.id | String | Mandatory | Pass the ID of an object to add as a relation. | | data.target.name | String | Optional | Pass the value of the object. | | data.target.sub_type | String | Optional | Pass the sub type of the object. | | data.target.type | String | Mandatory | Pass the object type. To get the list of supported object types, see _Supported SDO Types_ in Threat Data > Miscellaneous. | | data.relationship_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the relationship type.
Allowed value: `related-to` | #### Response Parameters Status code 200: Action Successfully Executed Status code 400: Bad Request (Check if you have passed valid data)

Add a rule on multiple threat data objects.

You can rules that are configured for manual execution only.

#### Request Parameter

| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| object_ids | Array | Mandatory | Pass the list of threat data object IDs to perform the bulk action. To retrieve a list of object IDs, use the _List Threat Data API_ under the Threat Data section. |
| object_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of threat data objects. To get the list of supported object types, see _Supported SDO Types_ in Threat Data > Miscellaneous. |
| data | JSON Object | Mandatory | Pass the additional data to pass with the request. |
| data.id | String | Mandatory | Pass the ID of a rule to run. To retrieve the list of rules, use the _List Rules API_ under the _Rules_ section. |

#### Response Parameters

Status code 200: Action Successfully Executed

Status code 400: Bad Request (Check if you have passed valid data)

item.name: Quick Preview

variable.description: [String] Pass the object type.
[String] Pass the ID of the object to preview.

request.description:
Retrieves specific details of a threat data object.

## Request Parameters

No parameter.

## Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| analyst_score | Integer | Returns the confidence score of a threat data object added by an analyst. |
| analyst_description | String | Returns the description of a threat data object added by an analyst. |
| analyst_tlp | String | Returns the TLP of a threat data object added by an analyst. |
| ctix_modified | Timestamp | Returns the object modification date in the CTIX platform. |
| ctix_created | Timestamp | Returns the creation date of the object in the CTIX platform. |
| type | String | Returns the object type. |
| created | Timestamp | Returns the creation date of the object as received from the source. |
| modified | Timestamp | Returns the modification date of the object as received from the source. |
| tlp | String | Returns the highest TLP as received from the source. |
| confidence_score | Integer | Returns the confidence score ranging between 0 to 100 as calculated by the CTIX confidence engine. |
| confidence_type | String | Returns the confidence score configuration type. |
| value | String | Returns the value of the object's main attribute. |
| primary_attribute | String | Returns the primary attribute of the custom object marked identifier in the CTIX platform. |
| sources | Array | Returns the list of sources from where the object has been received. |
| relation_count | Integer | Returns the count of the total relations that exist for the object in the CTIX platform. |

This section includes the APIs to retrieve IOC types, country details, source types, sources, and collections of the sources.

CTIX supports the following SDO types in Threat Data.

| SDO Type | SDO Key |

| Vulnerability | `vulnerability` |
| Malware | `malware` |
| Campaign | `campaign` |
| Threat Actor | `threat-actor` |
| Intrusion Set | `intrusion-set` |
| Attack Pattern | `attack-pattern` |
| Incident | `incident` |
| Course of Action | `course-of-action` |
| Identity | `identity` |
| Tool | `tool` |
| Infrastructure | `infrastructure` |
| Location | `location` |
| Malware Analysis | `malware-analysis` |
| Custom Objects | `custom-object` |
| Indicator | `indicator` |
| Note | `note` |
| Observed Data | `observed-data` |
| Opinion | `opinion` |
| Report | `report` |
| Observables | `observable` |

item.name: Saved Search

query.description: [Integer] Pass the page number of the saved searches for the user.
[Integer] Pass the page size for the page of saved search for the user.
[String] Pass the query text to search for a saved search.

request.description:
Lists the threat data queries that are saved by the user.

#### **Request Parameters**

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

#### **Response Parameters**

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| next | String | Returns a link to the next page. |
| prev | String | Returns a link to the previous page. |
| page_size | Integer | Returns the size of the requested page. |
| results | Array | Returns a list of saved searches. |
| id | String | Returns the unique ID of the saved search. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the saved search. |
| type | String | Returns the type of saved search as `basic` or `cql`. |
| query | String | Returns the CQL query of the saved search. |
| created_by | String | Returns the details of the user who created the saved search. |
| shared_type | String | Returns the privacy parameter of the saved search, such as `private`, `global`, `specific` `users`, or `systems`. |
| shared_users | Array | If the privacy parameter is `specific users,` then it returns the list of users with whom the saved search is shared. |
| editable | Bool | Returns `true` if the user has permission to edit the saved search, else returns `false`. |
| description | String | Returns the description of the saved search. |
| is_threat_data_search | Bool | Returns `true` if the saved search is a threat data search or copied from a reports search, else returns `false`. |
| meta_data | String | Returns the metadata of the saved search that helps in the transformation to the CQL query or threat data filters. |
| order | Integer | Returns the order of the saved search for the user in the listed saved search. |
| pinned | Bool | Returns `true` if the saved search is pinned by the user, else returns `false`. |

response.name: List Saved Searches

| type | String | Returns the type of saved search as `basic` or `cql`. |
| query | String | Returns the CQL query of the saved search. |
| created_by | String | Returns the details of the user who created the saved search. |
| shared_type | String | Returns the privacy parameter of the saved search, such as `private`, `global`, `specific` `users`, or `systems`. |
| shared_users | Array | If the privacy parameter is `specific users,` then it returns the list of users with whom the saved search is shared. |
| editable | Bool | Returns `true` if the user has permission to edit the saved search, else returns `false`. |
| description | String | Returns the description of the saved search. |
| is_threat_data_search | Bool | Returns `true` if the saved search is a threat data search or copied from a reports search, else returns `false`. |
| meta_data | String | Returns the metadata of the saved search that helps in the transformation to the CQL query or threat data filters. |
| order | Integer | Returns the order of the saved search for the user in the listed saved search. |
| pinned | Bool | Returns `true` if the saved search is pinned by the user, else returns `false`. |

variable.description: [String] Pass the unique ID of a saved search.

request.description:
Updates the saved search data by providing a valid ID.

#### **Request Parameters**

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of the saved search as basic or CQL. |
| name | String | Mandatory | Pass the name of the saved search. |
| query | String | Mandatory | Pass the dumped value of the query. |
| shared_type | String | Optional | Pass the privacy parameter of the saved search. It returns a value from private, global, specific users, or systems. |
| description | String | Optional | Pass the description of the saved search. |
| meta_data | String | Optional | Pass the metadata of the saved search that helps in the transformation to the CQL query or threat data filters. |

#### **Response Parameters**

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | UUID | Returns the unique ID of the saved search. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the saved search. |
| type | String | Returns the type of saved search as basic or CQL. |
| query | String | Returns the CQL query corresponding to the saved search. |
| created_by | String | Returns the name of the user who created the saved search. |
| shared_type | String | Returns the privacy parameter of the saved search. It returns a value from private, global, specific users, or systems. |
| shared_users | Array | Returns the list of the users that has access to the saved search, if specific users are selected as a privacy parameter. |
| editable | Bool | Returns True if the user has permission to edit the saved search, else returns False. |
| description | String | Returns the description of the saved search. |
| is_threat_data_search | Bool | Returns True if the saved search is either from threat data search or copied from reports type, else returns False. |
| meta_data | String | Returns the metadata of the saved search that helps in the transformation to the CQL query or threat data filters. |
| order | Integer | Returns the order of the saved search for the user in the listed saved search. |
| pinned | Bool | Returns True if the saved search is pinned by the user, else returns False. |

variable.description: [String] Pass the unique ID of a saved search.

request.description:
Deletes a saved search.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

#### Response Parameters

Status code 204: The saved search is deleted successfully.

Pins a saved search to the top.

#### Request Parameters

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| saved_search | String | Mandatory | Pass the ID of a saved search. |
| order | Integer | Optional | Pass the order in which you want the saved search to appear. |

#### Response Parameter

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| result | String | Returns success if the saved search is pinned successfully. Else, returns failure. |

Removes a saved search from the pinned search list.

#### Request Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| saved_search | String | Mandatory | Pass the ID of a pinned saved search. |

#### Response Parameters

Status code 204: The saved search is removed from the pinned search list successfully.

Updates the order of a pinned saved search.

#### Request Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| saved_search | String | Mandatory | Pass the ID of a pinned saved search. |
| order | Integer | Optional | Pass the order in which the saved search appears. |

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| result | String | Returns success if the saved search is pinned successfully. Else, returns failure. |

item.description: Save the frequently searched threat data elements and CQL queries. You can share a saved search with other users for use.

query.description: [Integer] Pass the page number to filter threat data objects by page number.
[Integer] Pass the number of records per page to filter the threat data objects by page size.
[Integer] Pass the number of threat data objects to be retrieved.
[Boolean] Pass true to retrieve the details of the last enrichment for the objects that can be enriched.
[String]

request.description:
Returns a list of threat data objects from the CTIX application. You can retrieve a maximum of 500,000 threat data objects using this API. #### **Request Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | query | String | Mandatory | Pass a CQL query to filter and fetch results. | #### **Response Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | analyst_score | String | Returns the score assigned to a threat data object by an analyst. | | analyst_tlp | String | Returns the TLP assigned to a threat data object by an analyst. | | confidence_score | Integer | Returns the score calculated by the CTIX confidence score engine. | | confidence_type | String | Returns the type of confidence.
`ctix`: Confidence score is calculated in CTIX.
`third-party`: Confidence score is calculated by a third-party application. | | country | String | Returns the country name where the threat data object was seen. | | created | Epoch | Returns the date and time of the creation of the threat data object. | | ctix_created | Epoch | Returns the date and time of the creation of the threat data object in CTIX. | | ctix_modified | Epoch | Returns the date and time of modification of the threat data object in CTIX. | | first_seen | Epoch | Returns the date and time at which the threat data object was first seen. | | id | String | Returns the ID of the threat data object. | | indicator_type | String | Returns the type of indicator.
Returns `null` if the threat data object is not an indicator. | | ioc_type | String | Returns the type of indicator.
Returns `null` if the threat data object is not an indicator. | | is_actioned | Bool | Returns True if an action was performed on the threat data object, else returns False. | | is_deprecated | Bool | Returns `true` if the threat data object is deprecated, else returns `false`. | | is_false_positive | Bool | Returns `true` if the threat data object is marked as false positive, else returns `false`. | | is_reviewed | Bool | Returns `true` if the threat data object is reviewed, else returns `false`. | | is_revoked | Bool | Returns `true` if the threat data object is revoked, else returns `false`. | | is_whitelisted | Bool | Returns `true` if the threat data object is marked as an allowed indicator, else returns `false`. | | last seen | Epoch | Returns the last seen date and time of the threat data object. | | modified | Epoch | Returns the modified date and time of the threat data object. | | name | String | Returns the name of the threat data object. | | primary_attribute | String | Returns the primary attribute of the threat data object if the threat data object is a custom object. | | published_collections | String | Returns the name of the collections in which the threat data object is published. | | severity | String | Returns the severity of the threat data object. | | source_collections | Array | Returns the list of IDs and names of source collections of the threat data object. | | source_confidence | String | Returns the confidence score of the threat data object as reported by its source. | | sources | Array | Returns the list of sources that reported this threat data object. | | sub_type | String | Returns the sub-type of an object if it is an indicator. | | tags | String | Returns tags defined on the threat data object. | | tlp | String | Returns the TLP assigned to the threat data object. | | valid_from | Epoch | Returns the date and time since when this threat data object is valid. | | valid_until | Epoch | Returns the date and time until when this threat data object is valid. | | enrichments | Dictionary | Returns the details of the last enrichment of the object if the object was enriched |
