item.description: The MITRE ATT&CK Navigator is a web-based tool for annotating and exploring ATT&CK matrices. It can be used to visualize defensive coverage, red/blue team planning, the frequency of detected techniques, and more. Navigator tactics include various strategies used by the threat actors whereas techniques define the way of achieving the strategies.

item.name: Fang/Defang

request.description:
Convert fanged data into defanged format and vice-versa. #### Request Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | text | String | Required | Pass the data to fang or defang. | | categorize | Boolean | Optional | Pass true to categorize the passed data into IOC types. Default: false | | type | String | Required | Pass one of the following:
`fang`: Converts defanged data into fanged format
`defang`: Converts fanged data into defanged format | #### **Response Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | raw_text | Array | Returns the fanged or defanged data in raw test format. | If `categorize = true` is passed in the request parameters, then the response includes the IOC types and the fanged or defanged data as key-value pairs.

item.description: Use Fang-Defang to neutralize malicious information with an obfuscated representation so that it is not dangerous if this information is inadvertently clicked or automatically processed in error.

item.name: Rules
Save Result Set

query.description:
Pass the Saved Result Set version. 
Allowed values: v2, v3.
Default value: v2
v2: Retrieves the data that is published using the Save Result Set action in rules. Saved Result Set v2 retrieves indicator and vulnerability data only.
v3: Retrieves the data that is published using the Save Result Set V3 action in rules.
Pass a tag name to filter data. All data associated the passed tag will be returned.
Pass the published time in EPOCH format from which you want to retrieve data.
Pass the published time in EPOCH format until which you want to retrieve data.
Pass the page number to retrieve records. Default value: 1
Pass the number of records to retrieve per page. Default value: 10

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

| --- | --- | --- |
| next | String | Returns a link to the next page. |

Save Result Set is a specification in CTIX that is designed to make CTIX data available over a web service such as OpenAPI.

Use Rules on CTIX to make indicators available over the Save Result Set.

Pass the ID of the creator of the rules to filter.
Pass the status of the rules. Allowed values: DRAFT, ACTIVE, INACTIVE. By default, rules with all status are retrieved.
Pass the last active time in EPOCH format to filter rules.

request.description:
Retrieves a list of rules from the platform.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any input parameter.

#### **Response Parameters**

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| next | String | Returns a link to the next page of the response. |
| page_size | Integer | Returns the number of rules retrieved per page. |
| previous | String | Returns a link to the previous page of the response. |
| total | Integer | Returns the total number of rules available in the platform. |
| id | String | Returns the rule ID. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the rule. |
| source | Array | Returns the name of the sources of the rule. |
| is_active | Boolean | Returns true if the rule is in active or draft state, else returns false. |
| last_activity_on | Timestamp | Returns the date and time at which the rule was last executed successfully. |
| ctix_created | Timestamp | Returns the date and time at which the rule was created. |
| ctix_modified | Timestamp | Returns the date and time at which the rule was last modified. |
| is_follow | Boolean | Returns true if the current user follows the rule, else returns false. |
| no_conditions | Boolean | Returns true if the rule is configured with no conditions, else returns false. |
| all_sources_and_collections | Boolean | Returns true if the rule is configured with all sources, else returns false. |
| status | String | Returns the status of the rule. |
| tags | Array | Returns the list of tags applied on a rule. |
| created_by | JSON Object | Returns the details of the user who created the rule. |
| updated_by | JSON Object | Returns the details of the user who last modified the rule. |
| trigger_on_update | Boolean | Returns true if the rule is configured to trigger on an update, else returns false. |

request.description:
Returns a list of rules with the ID and name of the rules only.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any input parameter.

#### **Response Parameters**

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | String | Returns the ID of the rule. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the rule. |
| next | String | Returns a link to the next page of the response. |
| page_size | Integer | Returns the number of rules retrieved per page. |
| previous | String | Returns a link to the previous page of the response. |

variable.description: Pass the unique ID of a rule.

request.description:
Retrieves the details of a rule.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any input parameter.

#### **Response Parameters**

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | String | Returns the ID of the rule. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the rule. |
| description | String | Returns the description of the rule. |
| actions | Array | Returns the list of actions applied to the rule. |
| rule | Array | Returns the list of conditions applied to the rule. |
| rule_source | Array | Returns the list of source objects of the rule. |
| no_conditions | Boolean | Returns true if no conditions are configured on the rule, else returns false. |
| is_active | Boolean | Returns true if the rule is active, else returns false. |
| status | String | Returns the status of the rule as Active, Inactive, or Draft. |
| tags | Array | Returns the list of tags applied to the rule. |
| created_by | String | Returns the ID of the user who created the rule. |
| updated_by | String | Returns the ID of the user who last modified the rule. |
| trigger_on_update | Boolean | Returns true if a rule is configured to trigger on an update, else returns false. |
| run_rule_after_enrich | Boolean | Returns true if the rule is configured to run after the enrichment policy, else returns false. |
| exclude_wl | Boolean | Returns true to exclude allowed threat data from passing in the rules, else returns false. |
| exclude_fp | Boolean | Returns true to exclude false-positive threat data from passing in the rules, else returns false. |
| is_follow | Boolean | Returns true if the current user follows the rule, else returns false. |
| ctix_created | Timestamp | Returns the time and date when the rule was created. |
| ctix_modified | Timestamp | Returns the time and date when the rule was last modified. |
| all_sources_and_collections | Boolean | Returns true if all sources are configured on the rule, else returns false. |

Update specific details of a rule. #### **Request Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | Description | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | name | String | Optional | Pass the name of the rule within 50 characters. | | description | String | Optional | Pass the description of the rule within 2000 characters. | | status | String | Optional | Pass the status of the rule.
Allowed values: DRAFT, ACTIVE, INACTIVE | | exclude_wl | Boolean | Optional | Pass true to exclude the allowed threat data from passing in the rule. Else pass false. | | exclude_fp | Boolean | Optional | Pass true to exclude the false-positive threat data from passing in the rule. Else pass false. | | tags | Array | Optional | Pass the list of tag IDs added to the rule. | | trigger_on update | Boolean | Optional | Pass true to trigger the rule after an update. Else pass false. | | is_active | Boolean | Optional | Pass true to activate the rule. Else pass false. | | retry_on_broken | Boolean | Optional | Pass true to retry running the rule after a failed attempt. Else pass false. | | is_follow | Boolean | Optional | Pass true to follow the rule. Else pass false. | | is_manual_run | Boolean | Optional | Pass true to enable manually running the rule. Else pass false. | | run_rule_after_enrichment | Boolean | Optional | Pass true to run the rule after every enrichment. Else pass false. | #### **Response Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | id | String | Returns the ID of the rule. | | name | String | Returns the name of the rule. | | description | String | Returns the description of the rule. | | actions | Array | Returns the list of actions applied to the rule. | | rule | Array | Returns the list of conditions applied to the rule. | | rule_source | Array | Returns the list of source objects of the rule. | | no_conditions | Boolean | Returns True if no conditions are configured on the rule, else returns False. | | is_active | Boolean | Returns true if the rule is active, else returns false. | | status | String | Returns the status of the rule as Active, Inactive, or Draft. | | tags | Array | Returns the list of tags applied to the rule. | | created_by | String | Returns the ID of the user who created the rule. | | update_by | String | Returns the ID of the user who last modified the rule. | | trigger_on_update | Boolean | Returns true if a rule is configured to trigger on an update, else returns false. | | run_rule_after_enrich | Boolean | Returns true if the rule is configured to run after the enrichment policy, else returns false. | | exclude_wl | Boolean | Returns true to exclude allowed threat data from passing in the rules, else returns false. | | exclude_fp | Boolean | Returns true to exclude false-positive threat data from passing in the rules, else returns false. | | is_follow | Boolean | Returns true if the current user follows the rule, else returns false. | | ctix_created | Timestamp | Returns the time and date in EPOCH format when the rule was created. | | ctix_modified | Timestamp | Returns the time and date in EPOCH format when the rule was last modified. | | all_sources_and_collections | Boolean | Returns true if all sources are configured on the rule, else returns false. |

request.description:
Returns a list of global tasks.

#### **Request Parameters**

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

#### **Response Parameters**

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Next | string | Returns the pagination link to the next page. |
| Prev | string | Returns the pagination link to the previous page. |
| Page_size | integer | Returns the number of elements that can be present on a page. |
| Results | json array | Returns the list of tasks. |
| id | string | Returns the ID of the task. |
| created | Timestamp | Returns the date of creation of the task. |
| modified | Timestamp | Returns the date the task was modified. |
| created_by | string | Returns the details of the user who created the task. |
| assignee | string | Returns the details of the assigned user of the task. |
| text | String | Returns the text or description of the task. |
| priority | String | Returns the priority of the task. |
| status | string | Returns the status of the task. Status can be in Progress, not started, or completed. |
| deadline | integer | Returns the deadline of the task. |
| closure comment | string | Returns any comments that are added while marking the task as complete. |
| completed_on | Timestamp | Returns the date of completion of the task. |
| type | string | Returns the type of threat data object that is associated with the task. |
| object ID | string | Returns the Threat data object ID associated with the task. |

request.description:
Creates a task in multiple threat data objects. #### Request Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | object_type | String | Required | Pass the type of the threat data object such as indicator, malware, vulnerability, and more. | | object_ids | Array | Required | Pass a list of threat data object IDs to associate the task. | | data | String | Required | Pass the data associated with the task. | | data: assignee | String | Required | Pass the ID of a user to assign the task. | | data: text | String | Required | Pass the description of the task to be performed. | | data: priority | String | Optional | Pass the priority of the task.
Allowed values:
`high`
`medium`
`low`
Default: `Medium` | | data: status | String | Optional | Pass the status of the task.
Allowed values:
`not_started`
`in_progress`
`completed`Default: `not_started` | | data: closure_comment | String | Optional
(Required if the status is `completed`) | Pass the comment to close the task. | | data: deadline | Integer | Optional | Pass the deadline of the task. | | data: type | String | Optional | Pass the type of the threat data object. For example, indicator, malware, vulnerability, campaign, and more. | #### Response Parameters Status code 200: Action Successfully Executed

request.description:
Request to create/start a STIX form detailed submission, the API returns a unique Identifier that will be used for all the subsequent calls in the create intel/detailed submission.

**Request Parameters:**

| **Parameter** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| title | String (Required): Specifies the title of the stix detailed submission |
| status | String (Required): Current status of the submission. |
| component | String (Required): Identifies the CTIX component, For detailed submission "stix-form" value is used. |
| enabled_stix_objects | List (Optional): specifies the STIX objects user can configure/modify for the current detailed submission. |

**Response Parameters:**

| **Parameter** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| id | String: Unique Identifier for the initiated detailed submission/create-intel. |
| status | String: current status of the detailed submission. |
| component | String: Identifies the CTIX component, for detailed submission to "stix-form" value is returned. |
| enabled_stix_objects | List: specifies the STIX objects user can configure/modify for the current detailed submission. |
| created | Timestamp: created timestamp for the submission |
| modified | Timestamp: modified timestamp for the submission |
| title | String: specifies the title of the stix detailed submission |

Updates the detailed STIX form submission that the user initiated.

#### Request Parameters

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| title | String | Mandatory | Pass the title of the detailed STIX form submission. |
| status | String | Mandatory | Pass the current status of the detailed STIX form submission. |
| component | String | Mandatory | Pass stix-form as the CTIX component. |
| enabled_stix_objects | Array | Optional | Pass the STIX objects a user can configure or modify for the current detailed STIX form submission. |
| server_collection | Array | Optional | Pass the name of the identifier and the collection in which the submission can be published. |

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | String | Returns the unique identifier for the initiated detailed STIX form submission. |
| created | epoch | Returns the date and time at which the detailed STIX form submission was created. |
| component | String | Returns stix-form as the CTIX component. |
| modified | epoch | Returns the date and time at which the detailed STIX form submission was modified. |
| title | String | Returns the title of the detailed STIX form submission. |
| status | String | Returns the current status of the detailed STIX form submission. |
| enabled_stix_objects | Array | Returns the list of STIX objects that a user configured or modified for the current detailed STIX form submission. |
| server_collection | Array | Returns the list of identifiers and names of collections in which the submission was published. |

request.description:
Adds an Indicator SDO in the initiated detailed STIX form submission.

#### Request Parameters

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| confidence | Integer | Optional | Pass the confidence score for the indicator ranging between 0 to 100. |
| created_by_ref | Array | Optional | Pass the list that contains the ID and name of the SDO being referred. |
| revoked | Bool | Optional | Pass true to specify if the SDO is revoked, else pass false. |
| labels | Array | Optional | Pass the list that contains the ID and name of the labels attached to the SDO. |
| name | String | Mandatory | Pass the name of the SDO. |
| description | String | Optional | Pass the description of the SDO. |
| custom_properties | Array | Optional | Pass the list that contains the unique identifier for the custom property, type, name, and the value to add for the same. |
| aliases | Array | Optional | Pass the list of strings that specify the also-known names for the SDO. |
| tlp | Array | Optional | Pass the list that contains the UI labels and value of the TLP. |
| kill_chain_phases | Array | Optional | Pass the list that contains the typical STIX standard kill chain name and the phase name for the same. |
| indicator_types | Array | Optional | Pass the list of STIX vocabulary for indicator types. |
| valid_from | epoch | Mandatory | Pass the date and time since when the SDO is valid. |
| valid_until | epoch | Mandatory | Pass the date and time till which the SDO is valid. |
| observables | Array | Optional | Pass the list that contains parameters, such as expressions and indicator patterns. |
| expressions | Array | Optional | Pass the list containing the STIX pattern expressions. |
| observation_operator | operator | Optional | Pass the operator connecting the comparison expressions. |
| comparison_expression | Array | Optional | Pass the list that specifies the operator and value of the SDO that is part of the expression with meta information like the UI label and the subtype. |
| external_references | Array | Optional | Pass the list that contains the details of the source acting as an external reference. |

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| created | epoch | Returns the date and time at which the SDO was created in the application. |
| modified | epoch | Returns the date and time at which the SDO was modified in the application. |
| id | String | Returns the unique identifier of the SDO. |

request.description:
Deletes all SDOs added to the detailed STIX form submission.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any input parameters.

item.description:
A Course of Action is an action taken either to prevent an attack or to respond to an attack that is in progress. It may describe technical, automatable responses (applying patches, reconfiguring firewalls) but can also describe higher -evel actions like employee training or policy changes. For example, a course of action to mitigate a vulnerability could describe applying the patch that fixes it.

The Course of Action SDO contains a textual description of the action; a reserved action property also serves as a placeholder for future inclusion of machine automatable courses of action.

item.description: The Location SDO is related to an Identity or Intrusion Set to indicate that the identity or intrusion set is located in that location. It is also related to a malware or attack pattern to indicate that they target victims in that location. The Location object describes geographic areas, not governments, even in cases where that area might have a government. For example, a Location representing the United States describes the United States as a geographic area, not the federal government of the United States.

item.description: Infrastructure refers to the physical components of interrelated systems providing commodities and services essential to enable, sustain, or enhance societal living conditions. Infrastructure can be fundamental facilities and systems serving a country, city, or other areas, including the services and facilities necessary for its economy to function. Infrastructure can also comprise public and private physical improvements such as roads, railways, bridges, tunnels, water supply, sewers, electrical grids, and telecommunications (including Internet connectivity and broadband speeds).

item.description: CTIX now supports accessing third-party repositories, such as Majestic Million, which provides a list of well-known IOCs.With access to millions of IOCs, CTIX can verify incoming data, enhancing the detection and segregation of well-known IOCs. These repositories also help filter commonly known allowed indicators, allowing focused research on relevant and targeted intelligence. Additionally, if an allowed indicator is reported as suspicious, CTIX offers an option to add it to the third-party ignored list.

query.description: Pass the page number to retrieve indicators. Default value: 1
Pass the number of records to retrieve per page. Default value: 10
Pass an allowed indicator type to filter result. To retrieve the supported list of allowed indicator types, se the GET List Supported Allowed Indicator Types API.
Pass the ID of a user to filter records by the creator.
Pass the ID of a user to filter records by the modifier.
Pass the created from time in EPOCH format to filter records based on the created time.
Pass the created to time in EPOCH format to filter records based on the created time.
Pass the last active from time in EPOCH format to filter records based on the last activity time.
Pass the last active to time in EPOCH format to filter records based on the last activity time.
Pass -created to sort result in descending order based on the created time. Pass created to sort result in ascending order.

| name | String | Mandatory | Pass the value of the keyword. |
| is_active | Boolean | Optional | Pass true to activate the keyword, else pass false to deactivate. |
| is_removed | Boolean | Optional | Pass true to remove the keyword, else pass false. |

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| created | Timestamp | Returns the date and time of the creation of a watchlist keyword. |
| created_by | String | Returns the ID of the user that created the watchlist keyword. |
| external_recipients | JSON | Returns the list of email addresses of the external recipients. |
| id | String | Returns the ID of the watchlist keyword. |
| internal_recipients | JSON | Returns the user details of the internal recipients. |
| is_active | Boolean | Returns true if the watchlist keyword is active, else returns false. |
| is_email_alert | Boolean | Returns true if an email alert is sent for the watchlist keyword, else returns false. |
| is_removed | Boolean | Returns true if the watchlist keyword is removed, else returns false. |
| last_activity_on | Timestamp | Returns the date and time at which the watchlist keyword was last active. |
| modified | Timestamp | Returns the date and time of modification of the watchlist keyword. |
| name | String | Returns the value of the watchlist keyword. |
| occurrences | Integer | Returns the number of occurrences found for a watchlist keyword. |
| updated_by | String | Returns the ID of the user that updated the watchlist keyword. |

item.description:
Widgets are the building blocks of a dashboard that consolidate the related data of one type and provide valuable information. A dashboard includes multiple widgets that are related to the use case of the dashboard. There are two types of widgets:

- **Summary Widgets**: Summary widgets display information about a specific entity based on the time frame selected for the dashboard. For example, Domain Objects.
- **Information Widgets**: Information widgets display a comparison among two or more entities based on the time frame selected for the dashboard. You can view data in various layouts, such as graphs, pie charts, line charts, and more. For example, Feed Sources vs IOCs.

request.description:
Retrieves all dashboards.

#### **Request Parameters**

This API endpoint does not require any input parameters.

#### **Response Parameters**

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| results | Array | Returns the list of the containing data. |
| results: dashboard_id | String | Returns the unique ID of the dashboard. |
| results: name | String | Returns the name of the dashboard. |
| results: description | String | Returns the description of the dashboard. |
| layout_type | Integer | Returns zero for a stationary dashboard or one for a rotating dashboard. |
| is_default | Bool | Returns True if it is a default dashboard, else returns False. |
| shared_type | String | Returns the privacy parameter of the saved search as global, private, or system. |
| rotation_timer | Integer | Returns the duration of rotation of widgets, when the layout of the dashboard is a rotating dashboard. |
| editable | Bool | Returns True if the dashboard is editable, else returns False. |
| created_by | JSON | Returns user details who created the dashboard, such as email, first name, user ID, and last name. |

request.description:
Creates a basic (standard) report in CTIX. > Do not use this endpoint to create advanced reports. #### Request Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | schedule | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the schedule details such as repeat type, start date and time, duration time, duration value, and repeat value. | | schedule: start_datetime | Timestamp | Mandatory | Enter the start time of the report in EPOCH format. | | schedule: repeat_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the frequency of the schedule.
Allowed values:
`once daily weekly monthly` | | schedule: ends_on | JSON Object | Mandatory | Pass the end time of the report. | | schedule: ends_on: type | String | Mandatory | Pass the schedule end type.
Allowed values:
`never end_date occurrences` | | schedule: ends_on: value | Timestamp (If the `ends_on: type` is `end_date`)
Integer (If the `ends_on: type` is `occurences`) | Mandatory | Pass the end time in EPOCH format or the number of occurences. | | schedule: duration_value | Integer | Mandatory | Pass the time frame in days to include the data in the report. | | saved_search | JSON Object | Mandatory | Pass the saved search or custom query details used for generating the report. | | saved_search: id
(To create a report using a saved search) | String | Mandatory | Pass the ID of a saved search to generate the report. | | saved_search: query
(To create a report using custom filters) | String | Mandatory | Pass the query to generate the report. | | saved_search: meta_data
(To create a report using custom filters) | String | Optional | Pass any metadata to include. | | query_key | String | Optional | Pass the key to sort data in the report:
`ctix_created` (Default): Data in the report is sorted based on the system created date.
`ctix_modified`: Data in the report is sorted based on the system modified date. | | name | String | Mandatory | Pass the name of the report. | | columns | Array of JSON objects | Mandatory | Pass the list of columns to be displayed in a basic report. | | file_types | Array | Mandatory | Pass the report type, such as `csv`, `xls`. | | type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of report to be created, such as standard or advanced.
Allowed value:
`basic` (to create standard reports) | | shared_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the share type value.
Allowed values:
`private`
`global` | | internal_recipients | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the list of internal recipients with whom to share the report. | | external_recipients | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the list of external recipients with whom to share the report. | #### Response Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | basic_report_type | String | Returns the type of basic report, such as saved or custom. | | schedule: id | String | Returns the unique ID of the schedule. | | schedule: repeat_type | String | Returns the time interval for the re-run of the report. | | schedule: start_datetime | Timestamp | Returns the starting date and time in EPOCH format from which the report captures the received data. | | schedule: repeat_value | Integer | Returns the schedule frequency. | | schedule: duration_type | String | Returns the end duration interval for the captured data. | | schedule: duration_value | Integer | Returns the duration in days to capture data in the report. | | query_key | String | Returns the sorting type in basic reports:
`ctix_created`: Data in the report is sorted based on the system created date.
`ctix_modified`: Data in the report is sorted based on the system modified date. | | name | String | Returns the name of the report. | | id | String | Returns the unique ID of the report. | | columns | Array of JSON objects | Returns the list of columns in a report. | | internal_recipients | JSON Object | Returns the list of internal recipients with whom to share the report. | | external_recipients | JSON Object | Returns the list of external recipients with whom to share the report. | | type | String | Returns the type of report, such as basic or advanced. | | file_types | Array | Returns the report formats, such as CSV, XLS. | | shared_type | String | Returns the shared type, such as global or private. | | date_last_run | Timestamp | Returns the date and time in EPOCH format at which the report was manually run. | | schedule_last_run | Timestamp | Returns the scheduled date and time in EPOCH fromat of the report generation. | | saved_search | JSON Object | Returns the saved search object for the corresponding report used for generating the report. | | created_by | JSON Object | Returns the details of the user who created the report. |

Retrieves a list of reports from the CTIX application. #### Request Parameters This API endpoint does not require any request parameter. #### Response Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | next | String | Returns the URL link to the next page. | | previous | String | Returns the URL link of the previous page. | | page_size | Integer | Returns the size of the requested page. | | results | JSON Object | Returns results in JSON objects for each report. | | schedule | JSON Object | Returns the details of the scheduled runs. | | schedule: id | String | Returns the unique ID of the schedule. | | schedule: repeat_type | String | Returns the time interval for the re-run of the report. | | schedule: start_datetime | Timestamp | Returns the starting date and time in EPOCH format from which the report captures the received data. | | schedule: repeat_value | Integer | Returns the number of times a report has to run during the provided interval. | | schedule: ends_on | JSON Object | Returns the end type, number of attempts left, and end time. | | schedule: duration_value | Integer | Returns the duration in days to capture data in the report. | | query_key | String | Returns the sorting type in basic reports:
`ctix_created`: Data in the report is sorted based on the system created date.
`ctix_modified`: Data in the report is sorted based on the system modified date. | | name | String | Returns the title of the report. | | id | String | Returns the unique ID of the report. | | columns | Array of JSON objects | Returns the list of columns in a basic report. | | internal_recipients | JSON Object | Returns the list of internal recipients with whom to share the report. | | external_recipients | JSON Object | Returns the list of external recipients with whom to share the report. | | type | String | Returns the type of report, such as a basic or advanced. | | file_types | Array | Returns the report format, such as CSV and XLS for basic reports and PDF for advanced reports. | | shared_type | String | Returns the shared type, such as global or private. | | date_last_run | Timestamp | Returns the date and time in EPOCH format at which the report was the last run. | | created_by | JSON Object | Returns the details of the user who created the report. | | modified_by | JSON Object | Returns the details of the user who last modified the report. |

`csv`
`xls` | | internal_recipients | JSON Object | Optional
(At least one internal or external recipient is required) | Pass the list of details of internal users to share the report. | | internal_recipients: to | Array | Optional | Pass the details of an internal user to share the report. | | internal_recipients: to: first_name | String | Optional | Pass the first name of the internal recipient. | | internal_recipients: to: last_name | String | Optional | Pass the last name of the internal recipient. | | internal_recipients: to: email | String | Optional | Pass the email address of the internal recipient. | | internal_recipients: to: id | String | Optional | Pass the unique identifier of the internal recipient. | | query_key | String | Mandatory | Pass the value to specify whether the report must run based on the created time or last modified time of threat data in CTIX. Allowed values are ctix_created and ctix_modified. | | starttime | Timestamp | Mandatory | Pass the start date and time in EPOCH format from which the report captures data available in CTIX. | | endtime | Timestamp | Mandatory | Pass the end date and time in EPOCH format until which the report captures data available in CTIX. | | external_recipients | JSON Object | Optional
(At least one internal or external recipient is required) | Pass the list of external users to share the report. | | external_recipients: to | Array | Optional | Pass the email addresses of the external recipients. | #### Response Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | result | String | Returns the following success message:

request.description:
Retrieves the run logs of a report.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| total | Integer | Returns the total number of run logs available for a report. |
| next | String | Returns the URL of the next page. |
| previous | String | Returns the URL of the previous page. |
| files | Array of JSON Object | Returns the file object that contains meta-information for generating the downloadable link of the report. |
| files: id | String | Returns the unique ID of the file. |
| files: type | String | Returns the type of file such as csv or xlsx. |
| files: url | String | Returns the download URL of the file. |
| files: name | String | Returns the name of the report. |
| id | String | Returns the unique ID of the run log. |
| run_type | String | Returns the run-type applied on the report, such as scheduled, instant. |
| last_run | epoch | Returns the date and time at which the file was last run. |
| file_types | Array | Returns the list of file types in a run log, such as CSV, and XLS. |
| download_link | JSON Object | Returns the URL to download the report. |

item.description:
Reports present complex data and insights in a more understandable and actionable format. Reports further empower analysts to share timely and relevant information with key stakeholders, such as management, security teams, and other relevant parties.

CTIX supports the following types of reports:

- **Standard Reports**: Leverage saved searches, CQL queries, and filters to access specific data sets and build reports based on the selected parameters.
- **Advanced Reports**: Leverage widgets, tables, queries, filters, and images to access data and present them in an understandable and visually appealing format.

exec: -epochtime is being send in expires parameter of params
-sign is generated and then passed for generating signature
-Key based hashing is done using HMAC-SHA1 Algorithm
-Finally the generated signature is URL Encoded so as to make all the special characters URL Safes
